county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Marion. Created 1820 from Delaware County. President Warren G. Harding home and newspaper (Marion Star) — Harding era records. Marion County Historical Society. Located in Congress Lands. Marion Steam Shovel Company records document workers. Popcorn industry heritage. Check Delaware County for pre-1824 organizational records. Strong Methodist and Catholic church records. Marion Cemetery Association records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1820. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Marion County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1820. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Marion County Court of Common Pleas Records
source_profiles.repository: Marion County Clerk of Courts, Marion
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1820. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Marion County Clerk of Courts, Marion / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Court of Common Pleas. Post-1906 copies at NARA. Ohio had massive European immigration — German, Irish, Welsh, English, Italian, Hungarian, Slovak, Polish, Czech, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Ohio naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Medina. Western Reserve county. Created 1812 from Portage County. A.I. Root Company (beekeeping) records. Medina County Historical Society. Medina Public Library Genealogy Room. Strong New England settler heritage — many from Connecticut and Vermont. Well-preserved early township records. Weymouth area early settlement. Check Portage County for pre-1818 organizational records. Amish settlements in southern portion.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1812. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1812. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Medina County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1812. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Medina County Court of Common Pleas Records
source_profiles.repository: Medina County Clerk of Courts, Medina
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1812. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Medina County Auditor, Medina / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. Ohio county auditor records from 1812. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes before 1820.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Ohio History Connection on microfilm

source_profiles.source_name: Connecticut Western Reserve Land Records — Medina County
source_profiles.repository: Western Reserve Historical Society, Cleveland / CT State Library
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Connecticut ceded its Western Reserve to the federal government in 1800, but settlers arrived from 1796. Connecticut Land Company records document original purchasers and settlers. Check Connecticut state records for settler origins.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at WRHS in Cleveland; Connecticut State Library also has copies

source_profiles.repository: Medina County Clerk of Courts, Medina / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Court of Common Pleas. Post-1906 copies at NARA. Ohio had massive European immigration — German, Irish, Welsh, English, Italian, Hungarian, Slovak, Polish, Czech, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Ohio naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.source_name: Amish and Mennonite Church Records — Medina County
source_profiles.repository: Heritage Historical Library, Aylmer ON / Mennonite Historical Library, Goshen IN / Local church records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Amish church district records, family group sheets, Budget newspaper correspondence, and Mennonite church records. Ohio has the largest Amish population in the world. Records may be in German/Pennsylvanian Dutch. Die Botschaft and The Budget newspapers document Amish community events.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Amish records remain in private family collections; Heritage Historical Library has compiled indexes

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Pomeroy. Created 1819 from Gallia and Athens. Ohio River county — steamboat and ferry records. Pomeroy is built along a narrow strip between the river and bluffs. Major coal mining county — mine records document Irish, Welsh, and Eastern European immigrants. Meigs County Pioneer and Historical Society. Racine and Syracuse area records. Check Gallia and Athens for pre-1819 records. Lead Creek mine disaster records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1819. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1819. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Meigs County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1819. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Meigs County Court of Common Pleas Records
source_profiles.repository: Meigs County Clerk of Courts, Pomeroy
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1819. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Meigs County Auditor, Pomeroy / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. Ohio county auditor records from 1819. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes before 1820.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Ohio History Connection on microfilm

source_profiles.source_name: Ohio Company of Associates Land Records — Meigs County
source_profiles.repository: Marietta College Archives / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: The Ohio Company of Associates (organized 1786 in Massachusetts) purchased 1.5 million acres in SE Ohio. Records document original purchasers, many from New England. Marietta was the first permanent settlement.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Marietta College Special Collections and Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and Abolitionist Records — Meigs County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / National Underground Railroad Freedom Center
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio was a free state under the Northwest Ordinance (1787). Court records include fugitive slave cases, abolitionist society minutes, and related legal proceedings. Anti-slavery newspapers published in Ohio are valuable genealogical sources.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Ohio History Connection

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Celina. Created 1820 from Darke County, organized 1824. Located in Congress Lands. Grand Lake St. Marys (built as a canal feeder reservoir) construction records. Very strong German Catholic heritage — Coldwater, Celina, St. Henry, and Maria Stein church records essential. Maria Stein Heritage Museum. Mercer County Historical Society. Miami & Erie Canal corridor. Check Darke County for early records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1820. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1820. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.repository: Mercer County Clerk of Courts, Celina
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1820. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. The Ohio & Erie Canal (Cleveland to Portsmouth) and Miami & Erie Canal (Cincinnati to Toledo) transformed Ohio's economy and brought many immigrants.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.source_name: German Catholic Parish Records — Mercer County
source_profiles.repository: Catholic Archdiocese of Cincinnati / Parish offices
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Baptism, marriage, and burial records from German Catholic parishes. Many parishes kept records in Latin or German. Parish registers often include parents' birthplaces in Germany. Archdiocese of Cincinnati central archives hold some consolidated records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Parish records generally well-preserved; some microfilmed by FamilySearch

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Troy. Created 1807 from Montgomery County. Located partly in Congress Lands. Troy was an important early Ohio town on the Great Miami River. Miami County Historical and Genealogical Society. Piqua is named for the Piqua Shawnee — Johnston Farm/Indian Agency records. Miami & Erie Canal corridor — canal era records. Hobart Brothers welding company records. Strong German and Scots-Irish heritage. Check Montgomery for pre-1807 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1807. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Woodsfield. Created 1813 from Belmont, Washington, and Guernsey. Named for James Monroe. Remote, hilly county in Appalachian Ohio — township records and church records critical. Switzerland of Ohio scenic area. Monroe County Historical Society. Oil and gas production records (1860s+). Swiss immigrant settlements. Check parent counties for pre-1813 records. Some of the most isolated communities in Ohio.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1813. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Dayton. Major city with extensive records. Dayton and Montgomery County Public Library has outstanding genealogy collection (one of the best in Ohio). Wright brothers connection. Dayton History (Carillon Park) holds local archives. National Cash Register (NCR) employee records. Significant African American community records — Paul Laurence Dunbar heritage. 1913 Great Flood devastated Dayton — some records lost, but flood relief records are genealogical goldmines. Located in Virginia Military District. German immigrant records extensive.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1803. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.source_name: Virginia Military District Land Warrants — Montgomery County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Library of Virginia / BLM-GLO
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Virginia military bounty land warrants issued to Virginia Revolutionary War veterans. The Virginia Military District covered south-central Ohio between the Scioto and Little Miami Rivers. Warrants often bought and sold — chain of ownership can trace families. BLM-GLO has original federal patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records survive at multiple repositories — Ohio History Connection, Library of Virginia, and NARA

source_profiles.repository: Montgomery County Clerk of Courts, Dayton / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Court of Common Pleas. Post-1906 copies at NARA. Ohio had massive European immigration — German, Irish, Welsh, English, Italian, Hungarian, Slovak, Polish, Czech, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Ohio naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and Abolitionist Records — Montgomery County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / National Underground Railroad Freedom Center
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio was a free state under the Northwest Ordinance (1787). Court records include fugitive slave cases, abolitionist society minutes, and related legal proceedings. Anti-slavery newspapers published in Ohio are valuable genealogical sources.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. The Ohio & Erie Canal (Cleveland to Portsmouth) and Miami & Erie Canal (Cincinnati to Toledo) transformed Ohio's economy and brought many immigrants.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio provided approximately 320,000 soldiers to the Union Army — third most of any state. Roster Commission records, enlistment papers, discharge records, and pension files. Ohio History Connection holds the Ohio Roster Commission records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ohio Roster Commission published 'Official Roster of the Soldiers of the State of Ohio in the War of the Rebellion' (12 volumes)

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in McConnelsville. Created 1817 from Washington, Muskingum, and Guernsey. Muskingum River county. Morgan County Historical Society. Morgan's Raid (1863) passed through — Civil War records. Malta and McConnelsville twin cities. Congress Lands survey system. Rural Appalachian county — church records (Methodist, Baptist, Presbyterian) are essential supplements. Oil and gas records (1860s+). Check parent counties for pre-1817 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1817. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1848 from Knox, Marion, Delaware, Richland.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Mount Gilead. Created 1848 from Knox, Marion, Delaware, and Richland — check all four parent counties for pre-1848 records. Morrow County Historical Society. Located in Congress Lands. Late formation means earlier records scattered. Mount Gilead named for biblical reference. Agricultural county. Cardington and Edison area records. Strong Methodist and Baptist church records. Rural school district records important.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1848. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Zanesville. One of Ohio's earliest counties (1804). Zanesville was Ohio's state capital (1810-1812). Located in the Congress Lands (U.S. Military District). Zanesville was a major pottery and ceramics center — factory records document worker families. Y-Bridge (unique) crossing the Muskingum and Licking Rivers. Muskingum County Historical Society. Zane Grey birthplace. Ohio & Erie Canal and later Muskingum River improvement records. Strong early Presbyterian and Methodist records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1804. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
